findings: Provided Fleksy SDK repo README advertises custom views on top of the keyboard.
iOS KeyboardViewController exposes PanelConfiguration.heightTopBar = 50 and comments about customView/heightCustom deprecated constructor.

Android SampleKeyboardService and Flutter AndroidKeyboardService expose normal KeyboardConfiguration only; no top row, hotkey, shortcut, custom button, custom view, or CustomizationBundle implementation found in samples.
Repo appears to ship sample integrations plus proprietary Fleksy binary SDK/core artifacts, not the full source of the old Fleksy app Hotkey Extension UI.
External web result corroborates old Fleksy app Hotkey Extension existed, but not as visible source in this provided repo.
payload.prd_implication: PRD should treat the Fleksy feature as behavior to recreate on top of Unexpected Keyboard unless user can provide APK/source for exact app implementation.

apk_evidence.display_behavior: typed phrase auto-generates 1-2 character preview label unless user chooses icon
apk_evidence.runtime: ExtensionBar.HotKeys supplies HotKey(index, icon, phrase) list to HotKeyExtensionBar
apk_evidence.storage: hotkey_prefs entries keyed by index with JSON [isFont, icon, phrase]

payload.scope_refinement: Fleksy-inspired feature is custom snippets launched via top-row buttons; do not clone full Fleksy extension/store system.

answer.question: How should custom snippet buttons be organized in the top row?
answer.rationale: Show 7 snippet buttons at a time, with additional groups/pages if the user adds more; matches the APK Hotkey Extension model.

answer.answer: Fleksy-style groups of 7 with horizontal left/right scrolling between groups
answer.question: How should custom snippet buttons be organized in the top row?
answer.rationale: Each visible page shows seven snippet buttons; users swipe/scroll left or right to reveal additional groups of seven, matching the intended Fleksy-like interaction.

answer.question: How should users create and manage custom snippets?
answer.rationale: Use fixed indexed slots, phrase field, auto-generated preview label, optional icon-style label selection, and empty slots shown in the grid.

answer.question: When a user taps a custom snippet button, what should happen?
answer.rationale: Immediately commit the snippet text into the active app text field and leave the keyboard open, matching standard custom snippet key behavior.
